message:
test: coverage boost across process, config, health, robot, cli packages

Add GetProcessState, IsChildAlive alias, and processStateNames to process
package to fix cross-package API mismatches with robot callers. Update
robot/exit_sequences.go, robot/status_enrich.go, robot/health.go, and
resilience/monitor_test.go for new process API signatures. Add ShellPID
field to health.AgentHealth struct.

Coverage improvements:
- config: 84.2% → 90.2% (GetValue, ValidateDCGConfig, ValidateRanoConfig,
  applyEnvOverrides, dirWritable)
- process: 29.3% → 90.2% (GetProcessState, IsChildAlive, processStateNames)
- health: 64.8% → 65.9% (detectActivity/detectProcessStatus edge cases)
- robot: 48.7% → 48.9% (HardKillResult, isRobotErrorLine, TrendTracker,
  DiagnoseOutput, MonitorConfig)
- cli: pure function tests for 13 truncate variants, stripANSI, padRight,
  styled text helpers, splitAndTrim, getUnlocksDescription, AnnotatePrompt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ok7rj","title":"Implement baseline capture for probe methods","description":"Implement baseline capture functionality that all probe methods rely on.

## Purpose

All probe methods need to capture pane content before and after sending probe input.
This task implements the shared baseline capture logic.

## Implementation

```go
type PaneBaseline struct {
    Content      string    // Full pane content
    ContentHash  string    // For quick comparison
    CursorLine   int       // If available from tmux
    CursorCol    int       // If available
    CapturedAt   time.Time
}

func capturePaneBaseline(session string, pane int) (*PaneBaseline, error) {
    // Use tmux capture-pane with cursor position if possible
    // Format: -p for content, -P for formatting info
    target := fmt.Sprintf("%s:1.%d", session, pane)
    
    // Capture content
    cmd := exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "capture-pane", "-t", target, "-p")
    content, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("baseline capture failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    return &PaneBaseline{
        Content:     string(content),
        ContentHash: sha256Sum(content),
        CapturedAt:  time.Now(),
    }, nil
}

func comparePaneState(baseline, current *PaneBaseline) PaneChange {
    if baseline.ContentHash == current.ContentHash {
        return PaneChange{Changed: false}
    }
    
    // Compute diff for detailed analysis
    return PaneChange{
        Changed:    true,
        LinesDelta: countLineDiff(baseline.Content, current.Content),
    }
}
```

## Edge Cases

- Empty pane content (new session)
- Binary content in pane (rare)
- Very large pane content (truncation)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:44:20.334752523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:41:44.792628036Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:41:44.792582911Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ok7rj","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-oqlqm","title":"Task: Unit tests for Webhook System","description":"Unit tests for Webhook System (bd-3s2f).

## Test Coverage Areas

### 1. Webhook Configuration Tests
- Test valid minimal config (URL + events)
- Test valid full config with secret, timeout, retries
- Test HTTP rejected (HTTPS required)
- Test localhost HTTP allowed
- Test no events rejected
- Test unknown event type rejected

### 2. Webhook Payload Tests
- Test JSON marshaling with all fields
- Test signature computation is deterministic
- Test signature validation with correct/incorrect secret

### 3. Event Filter Tests
- Test exact match
- Test wildcard prefix (agent.*)
- Test wildcard all (*)
- Test multiple filters
- Test no match

### 4. Delivery Queue Tests
- Test enqueue and dequeue order
- Test queue overflow drops oldest

### 5. Retry Logic Tests
- Test exponential backoff delays
- Test capped at max delay
- Test shouldRetry for 5xx status codes
- Test no retry for 4xx client errors

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Test file exists at internal/webhook/webhook_test.go
2. Coverage reaches 80% for webhook package
3. Config validation tests cover all rules
4. Signature tests verify HMAC-SHA256
5. Event filter tests cover all wildcard patterns
6. Queue tests verify ordering and overflow behavior
7. Retry tests verify exponential backoff
8. All tests pass with go test ./...","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests for webhook creation
2. Unit tests for event delivery
3. Unit tests for retry logic
4. Unit tests for authentication
5. Test coverage at least 80 percent
6. Tests run under 10 seconds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:23:42.599512366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:15:56.341190341Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:15:56.341124488Z","close_reason":"Added webhook unit tests (http/https validation, agent.* wildcard, queue overflow drops oldest, HMAC deterministic) and hardened dispatcher behavior","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oqlqm","depends_on_id":"bd-1nna","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-oqlqm","depends_on_id":"bd-2c8y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-oqlqm","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-oqlqm","depends_on_id":"bd-349x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-oqlqm","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-p2pr8","title":"Sub-Epic: Critical Low Coverage Packages (<30%)","description":"# Critical Low Coverage Packages

## Scope
Packages with coverage below 30% requiring immediate attention:
- **git**: 2.0% - Git integration utilities
- **gemini**: 19.8% - Gemini CLI agent adapter
- **auth**: 27.4% - Authentication/authorization
- **serve**: 27.6% - HTTP server and API handlers
- **cli**: 27.8% - Command-line interface (largest package)

## Analysis & Approach

### git (2.0%)
- **Why low**: Almost entirely exec-based (runs git commands)
- **Strategy**: Test pure helpers (path parsing, commit message formatting)
- **Target**: 20-30% (limited by external dependency)

### gemini (19.8%)
- **Why low**: Network I/O to Gemini API, state machine logic
- **Strategy**: Test state parsing, response formatting, config validation
- **Target**: 40-50%

### auth (27.4%)
- **Why low**: Browser auth flows, tmux session monitoring
- **Strategy**: Test detection functions, credential parsing
- **Target**: 45-55%

### serve (27.6%)
- **Why low**: HTTP handlers, WebSocket, SSE
- **Strategy**: httptest for handlers, pure helpers already partly tested
- **Target**: 50-60%

### cli (27.8%)
- **Why low**: Massive package (~50 files), mostly cmd execution
- **Strategy**: Test pure helpers (formatting, filtering, validation)
- **Target**: 35-45% (incremental)

## Priority Rationale
These packages handle critical functionality (auth, API, CLI) and low coverage creates regression risk during refactoring.

 {"id":"bd-p3en","title":"Task: Define command tier taxonomy and categorization","description":"## Overview
Create the foundational tier taxonomy that categorizes all NTM commands into proficiency levels.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go defining:
   - Tier enum (Apprentice=1, Journeyman=2, Master=3)
   - CommandTier struct with {Command, MinTier, Description, Examples}
   - Global registry of all commands with their tier assignments

2. Tier 1 (Apprentice) - Essential workflow:
   - spawn: Create agent sessions
   - send: Send prompts to agents
   - status: View running sessions
   - kill: Stop sessions
   - help: Get help (tier-aware)

3. Tier 2 (Journeyman) - Full standard:
   - All Tier 1 commands
   - dashboard: Interactive TUI
   - context: View context window usage
   - tail: View agent output
   - interrupt: Send Ctrl+C to agents
   - swarm: Swarm orchestration
   - All standard flags

4. Tier 3 (Master) - Advanced/experimental:
   - All Tier 2 commands
   - --robot-* flags for automation
   - Internal debugging commands
   - Experimental features

## Implementation Notes
- Use Go constants for tier values
- Include help text per tier explaining what unlocks next
- Document promotion criteria in tier definitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- taxonomy.go compiles and exports tier constants
- All existing commands have tier assignments
- Tier progression is logical (each tier builds on previous)
- Unit tests verify taxonomy completeness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:07.875431526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700671521Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:03:20.700604966Z","close_reason":"Implemented command tier taxonomy in internal/cli/tiers/taxonomy.go with Tier enum, CommandInfo struct, and complete Registry mapping all commands","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p3en","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-p6quz","title":"Feature: Robot API - JSON interface for ensemble automation","description":"# Feature: Robot API

## Purpose
Expose ensemble functionality through JSON API for AI-to-AI orchestration and automation pipelines. Follows existing NTM robot mode patterns.

## Scope

### Robot Commands

**--robot-ensemble=SESSION**
Get ensemble state for a session.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "ensemble": {
    "session": "myproject",
    "question": "What architectural problems exist?",
    "preset": "architecture-review",
    "status": "active",
    "started_at": "2026-01-23T10:25:00Z",
    "modes": [
      {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "pane": "myproject__cc_1", "agent_type": "claude", "status": "done"}
    ],
    "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative", "status": "not_started"}
  },
  "summary": {"total_modes": 5, "completed": 2, "working": 2, "pending": 1}
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION**
Create new ensemble session.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='What problems exist?' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

**--robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION**
Trigger synthesis.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=myproject \
  --strategy=deliberative \
  --format=markdown
```
Returns synthesis output metadata or status.

**--robot-ensemble-modes**
List available modes.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "modes": [
    {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "name": "Abductive Reasoning", "category": {"code": "B", "name": "Ampliative"}}
  ],
  "categories": [{"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-presets**
List available ensembles (presets).
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "presets": [
    {"name": "architecture-review", "display": "Architecture Review", "description": "...", "modes": [{"id":"systems-thinking","code":"F43"}], "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative"}, "budget": {"total": 30000}, "allow_advanced": false, "tags": ["architecture"]}
  ]
}
```

### Modifiers (Unprefixed)
- **Spawn:** `--question`, `--preset`, `--modes`, `--agents`, `--assignment`, `--allow-advanced`, `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`, `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`, `--project`
- **Modes list:** `--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`
- **Synthesize:** `--strategy`, `--format`, `--output`, `--force`

### Strategy Set
Uses canonical set from bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).

### Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 1: Error (with error_code in JSON)
- 2: Feature unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All robot commands return valid JSON
- [ ] Follows existing robot envelope pattern (success/timestamp, optional error fields on failure)
- [ ] Exit codes are consistent
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] not nil
- [ ] Error codes are documented
- [ ] _agent_hints included where helpful

## Technical Notes
- Follow patterns in internal/robot/
- Add to capabilities.go for discoverability
- Ensure envelope compliance (see envelope_test.go)
- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per docs/robot-api-design.md","notes":"All 6 robot ensemble commands fully implemented and wired: --robot-ensemble, --robot-ensemble-spawn, --robot-ensemble-modes, --robot-ensemble-presets, --robot-ensemble-synthesize (implicit via flag), --robot-ensemble-suggest, --robot-ensemble-stop. Tests exist in ensemble_presets_test.go, ensemble_synthesize_test.go, ensemble_envelope_test.go, ensemble_modes_test.go.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:26:15.832578580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:20:38.143912625Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:20:38.143768347Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pjjk7","title":"EPIC: Canonical Assignment Flow (ntm assign + spawn --assign)","description":"## Goal
Reduce user confusion by providing **one** canonical assignment flow and **one** convenience entry point.

## Decision
- **Keep**: `ntm assign` as the only assignment command.
- **Keep**: `ntm spawn --assign` as the only convenience entry (spawn + assign).
- **Expose**: `--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency|round-robin` on `ntm assign`.
- **Drop/Merge**: `ntm swarm`, `ntm send --distribute`, YAML swarm config, and from‑file assignments.

## Why
Multiple entry points doing the same thing (assign work to agents) multiplies docs, tests, and code paths and will confuse users. The project benefits from a single, well‑designed path.

## Scope
- Keep existing strategies (`balanced`, `speed`, `quality`, `dependency`) intact and documented.
- Add `round‑robin` as an **optional** additional strategy (no renaming of existing ones).
- Implement assignment tracking + reporting so `balanced` can be truly load‑aware.
- Implement `ntm spawn --assign` as a thin wrapper over `ntm assign`.
- Update docs/help to emphasize canonical flow.

## Non‑Goals
- No new orchestration commands outside `assign` + `spawn --assign`.
- No YAML swarm config or additional CLI entry points.

## Success Criteria
- `ntm assign` is the only path for distributing work.
- `ntm spawn --assign` works end‑to‑end with clear output.
- Redundant commands are closed/dropped.
- Strategy list in docs/help matches code.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:08.450596296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:32.691425958Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pjjk7","depends_on_id":"bd-22nh8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-pjjk7","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
